page: Microsoft Azure F1 is a virtual machine instance within the F family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated vCPU mapped to one physical core on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Local storage is limited to a bundled 16 GB volume, and the instance does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the F1 instance in the lower performance tiers. Both single-core and multi-core CPU stress-ng tests yield a score of 804.38, ranking in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only small block cached transfers reaching 75.04 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write speeds drop to 6.86 GB/sec and 5.25 GB/sec respectively. Cryptographic and compression tasks show weak to poor performance, with gzip compression at 19.76 GB/sec and a Passmark CPU score of 1187.46.

Given the single-core architecture and 2.0 GB memory limit, this instance faces significant resource constraints. It is not capable of handling memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference, as demonstrated by its poor Redis SET throughput of 1.22 million operations per second and low 135M model inference speeds. The F1 instance is best utilized for lightweight, non-critical workloads such as basic static web serving, simple scripting, or low-demand development and testing environments where resource cost efficiency is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Microsoft Azure F1 is an entry-level virtual machine from the F family, powered by a single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 core running at 2.3 GHz. It features 2.0 GB of RAM, a 16 GB local storage volume, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Lacking GPU acceleration and hyperthreading, its hardware profile is designed for minimal resource requirements. Benchmark data indicates poor overall performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth metrics consistently placing in the bottom 10% of tested servers. The instance achieves a Passmark CPU score of 1187.46 and a static web serving throughput of 1.05 GB/sec. Due to these severe resource constraints, the F1 is best suited for lightweight utility tasks, basic static web hosting, and low-demand development or testing environments where qualitative cost efficiency is more critical than processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F1 is a single-core virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 16 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F1 is a 1-vCPU virtual machine with 2.0 GB RAM and 16 GB local storage, built for basic, low-demand utility workloads.
tagline: A single-core, low-memory virtual machine designed for basic utility tasks, lightweight scripting, and entry-level development environments.
bullet_points: Single dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 core running at 2.3 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory and 16 GB of local storage
Poor benchmark performance, placing in the bottom 10% for CPU and memory tests
No hyperthreading, GPU acceleration, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for static web serving, basic scripting, and lightweight testing
